synthesis.content: The Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) was incorporated after emerging from the defunct National Electric Power Authority (NEPA) as a product of the merger of the Transmission and Operations sectors. This incorporation occurred after the merger on April 1, 2004, and was followed by the enactment of the Electric Power Sector Reform (EPSR) Act. The TCN incorporation and EPSR Act enactment are related events in the context of Nigeria's national grid access dimension.

sentences: The Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) was incorporated after emerging from the defunct National Electric Power Authority (NEPA) as a product of the merger of the Transmission and Operations sectors.
This incorporation occurred after the merger on April 1, 2004, and was followed by the enactment of the Electric Power Sector Reform (EPSR) Act.
The TCN incorporation and EPSR Act enactment are related events in the context of Nigeria's national grid access dimension.
alternate_contents: The Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) was incorporated after emerging from the defunct National Electric Power Authority (NEPA) as a product of the merger of the Transmission and Operations sectors. This incorporation occurred after the merger on April 1, 2004, and was followed by the enactment of the Electric Power Sector Reform (EPSR) Act. The TCN incorporation and EPSR Act enactment are related events in the context of Nigeria's national grid access dimension.

The Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) was incorporated and emerged from the defunct National Electric Power Authority (NEPA) as a product of the merger of the Transmission and Operations sectors, with the Electric Power Sector Reform (EPSR) Act being enacted. TCN's incorporation and the EPSR Act both relate to the development of Nigeria's grid access. The TCN emerged from NEPA on April 1, 2004, and was later incorporated. The EPSR Act was enacted in the same year TCN was incorporated.
